data.description:

SIM Technology Group ( (HK:2000) ) has provided an announcement.

SIM Technology Group has called its annual general meeting for 5 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the accompanying directors’ and auditors’ reports. The agenda also includes re-election of three directors, approval for the board to set directors’ pay, re-appointment of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor, and a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of existing share capital, with an additional allowance tied to any future share repurchases, which could enhance the company’s financial flexibility and capital-raising capacity.

More about SIM Technology Group

SIM Technology Group Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the technology sector and is best known for providing electronic and communications solutions. The company targets both domestic and international markets, leveraging its listing on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong to access capital and support its ongoing development and expansion.

data.description:

HKT and HKT ( (HK:6823) ) has provided an announcement.

HKT Trust and HKT Limited announced that the audited consolidated financial statements for HKT Group Holdings and Hong Kong Telecommunications for the year ended 31 December 2025 have been published on the Singapore Exchange and Taipei Exchange websites. The disclosure underscores the importance of these subsidiaries to the group’s performance, with HKT Group Holdings generating nearly all of HKT Limited’s 2025 revenue and Hong Kong Telecommunications contributing about half, highlighting their central role in the company’s operational and regional growth strategy.

The announcement also clarifies the corporate structure, noting that Hong Kong Telecommunications is wholly owned by HKT Group Holdings, which in turn is wholly owned by HKT Limited. By providing transparency on revenue contribution and business focus across technology, telecoms and related services, the group reinforces its positioning as a leading Hong Kong-based communications and digital services operator with significant exposure to enterprise, consumer and emerging digital businesses.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6823) stock is a Buy with a HK$13.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on HKT and HKT stock, see the HK:6823 Stock Forecast page.

More about HKT and HKT

HKT Trust and HKT Limited, through HKT Group Holdings and Hong Kong Telecommunications, operate as a major technology and telecommunications provider. The groups offer enterprise solutions, mobile services, total home solutions, media entertainment, loyalty programmes, financial services and healthtech, with operations centred in Hong Kong and extending into mainland China and other international markets.

Shenzhen Expressway Co ( (HK:0548) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shenzhen Expressway said toll collection will cease on the Shuiguan Expressway section from Shuijingcun to Guanjingtoucun in Shenzhen at 24:00 on 25 April 2026, following directives from provincial and municipal authorities. The road is operated by Shenzhen Qinglong Expressway Co., in which Shenzhen Expressway holds a 50% stake, and Qinglong will hand over the related assets in line with transport authority rules.

The company stated that the end of toll operations on this section is not expected to have a material impact on its overall financial position or operating results. Investors are directed to past financial reports and monthly toll revenue disclosures for historical data on Shuiguan Expressway’s performance, suggesting that the asset’s contribution is relatively limited within the group’s broader expressway portfolio.

More about Shenzhen Expressway Co

Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited is a Chinese toll-road operator based in Shenzhen, focusing on the investment, construction, and operation of expressways and related infrastructure in the People’s Republic of China. Through subsidiaries and joint ventures, the company participates in regional transportation projects that support urban connectivity and long-term traffic development in the Greater Bay Area.

data.title: Top Form Renews Key Connected Supply Deal With Major Shareholder VDV
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Top Form International Limited ( (HK:0333) ) just unveiled an update.

Top Form International Limited has entered into a seventh renewal agreement with its substantial shareholder VDV to continue supplying ladies’ intimate apparel for a further three years from 1 July 2026 to 30 June 2029. The arrangement extends a long-standing commercial relationship under a master agreement governing recurring sales to VDV.

Because VDV holds about 25.66% of Top Form and is controlled by non-executive director Herman Van de Velde, the renewed supply arrangement is classified as a continuing connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules. As certain percentage ratios exceed 5%, the deal requires reporting, public announcement, independent shareholders’ approval and annual review, and an Independent Board Committee and financial adviser have been appointed, with a circular to be sent to shareholders by 15 May 2026.

The transactions are considered revenue in the ordinary and usual course of business and are exempt from separate transaction classification under another chapter of the Listing Rules. The renewal reinforces the stability of Top Form’s revenue base from a key shareholder-customer while subjecting the relationship to enhanced governance and minority shareholder safeguards through independent review and approval mechanisms.

More about Top Form International Limited

Top Form International Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the apparel manufacturing industry with a focus on ladies’ intimate apparel. The group supplies these products to brand and retail partners, including VDV, a substantial shareholder and long-term customer, reflecting its role in the global lingerie supply chain.

data.title: CGN Mining Posts Solid Q1 2026 Uranium Output and Active Trading Position
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

CGN Mining Co ( (HK:1164) ) just unveiled an update.

CGN Mining reported that uranium deposits in which it holds interests produced 580.9 tonnes of uranium in the first quarter of 2026, achieving 97.1% of the quarterly production plan. Output was led by its 49%-owned Kazakhstan associate Ortalyk at 426.4 tonnes, while Semizbay-U produced 154.5 tonnes, with a sulfuric acid shortage temporarily weighing on the Semizbay mine but not expected to affect full-year production.

On the trading side, the group held 967 tonnes of uranium inventory at the end of March 2026 at an average cost of US$80.60 per pound U3O8, alongside contracted but undelivered sales of 2,519 tonnes at an average price of US$89.20 per pound. New contracts and settlements in the quarter show active two-way trading, suggesting stable demand and pricing for its uranium portfolio, while management cautioned that the unaudited figures may fluctuate with market and operational conditions.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1164) stock is a Buy with a HK$3.67 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CGN Mining Co stock, see the HK:1164 Stock Forecast page.

More about CGN Mining Co

CGN Mining Company Limited is a Hong Kong-listed uranium producer and trader incorporated in the Cayman Islands, with core operations focused on natural uranium assets in Kazakhstan. The group holds minority interests in Semizbay-U LLP and Mining Company Ortalyk LLP, supporting its role in the global nuclear fuel supply chain through upstream production and physical uranium trading.

data.title: Giordano lifts Q1 revenue on retail and online gains despite GCC and wholesale drag
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Giordano International ( (HK:0709) ) has shared an update.

Giordano International reported a 3.9% year-on-year increase in group revenue to HK$1.04 billion for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, driven by a 7.4% rise in retail revenue and an 8.2% gain in same-store sales, despite a net reduction in store count. Growth was led by Greater China, where revenue climbed 7.8% on recovering demand in Hong Kong and Taiwan and strong online sales in Mainland China, and by Southeast Asia, where Singapore and Thailand outperformed.

Online revenue surged 40.5% across most markets, underscoring the group’s strategic emphasis on digitalisation under its five-year “Beyond Boundaries” plan, including planned upgrades to web and app interfaces to enhance the customer journey. This strength offset a 19.1% decline in wholesale revenue linked to inventory right-sizing at the South Korean joint venture and streamlining of Mainland China franchise operations, as well as a 5.2% revenue drop in the GCC due to weaker sentiment from regional tensions, with underlying growth stronger when excluding that drag.

More about Giordano International

Giordano International Limited is a Hong Kong-listed apparel retailer focused on casual wear, operating a network of stores and online channels across Greater China, Southeast Asia, Australia, and the Gulf Cooperation Council markets. The group sells its products through a mix of self-operated retail outlets, e-commerce platforms, and wholesale arrangements with overseas and Mainland China franchisees, aiming to expand its global brand presence.

China State Construction International Holdings reported unaudited group revenue of about RMB20.79 billion and joint-venture revenue of RMB734.97 million for the first quarter of 2026, down from RMB22.89 billion and RMB994.67 million a year earlier. data.title: Elegance Optical Faces Ongoing Trading Suspension After HKEX Review
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited ( (HK:0907) ) has provided an update.

Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited has been informed that the Listing Review Committee of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange has upheld an earlier decision to suspend trading in its shares, citing the company’s failure to maintain sufficient operations and assets to justify continued listing. The committee found that Elegance Optical’s recently revived eyewear business lacks a proven, coherent plan and sustainable track record, and that its new liquor trading venture is thin on signed contracts, overly reliant on a part-time consultant and unsupported by robust financial data, leaving the company’s asset base and financial position too weak to meet listing requirements.

In its review, the committee highlighted inconsistencies in Elegance Optical’s business model disclosures, questioned the viability of its profit forecasts, and noted that a key shareholder loan was not on normal commercial terms, underscoring concerns over the company’s financial health. As a result, the regulator concluded that, as of the February hearing, Elegance Optical had not demonstrated sufficient operational substance or asset value, prompting the continuation of the trading suspension and signalling heightened risk and uncertainty for shareholders and potential investors.

More about Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited

Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, historically operates in the eyewear sector and has recently re-focused on its eyewear business while also venturing into a liquor trading business. The company has sought to adopt an asset-light and branded model in eyewear and to build a new revenue stream through liquor trading, but both lines remain at an early stage with limited track record and unclear long-term sustainability.

The move underscores active shareholder engagement in capital allocation and could influence RUSAL’s dividend policy and cash distribution priorities.

The board plans to meet on 24 April 2026 to review the requisition and decide on calling the meeting, with a further session on 5 May 2026 to consider EGM-related matters including any dividend recommendation. The record date for shareholders’ entitlement to participate in the proposed EGM is expected to be 7 May 2026, signaling a near-term timeline for potential decisions that may impact investor returns and the company’s financial planning.

More about United Co RUSAL

United Company RUSAL is an international public joint-stock company incorporated in Jersey and continued in the Russian Federation, with its shares listed in Hong Kong and on Russian exchanges under the code RUAL. The company operates in the metals sector and is one of the world’s major aluminium producers, serving industrial and commercial customers across global markets.

data.title: Jiangsu Expressway Boosts Capital in Danjin Subsidiary to Advance Fuli Expressway Project
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jiangsu Expressway Co ( (HK:0177) ) just unveiled an update.

Jiangsu Expressway has approved a further cash capital injection of RMB128.18 million into its subsidiary Jiangsu Danjin Expressway Co., which is responsible for the Danyang to Jintan section of the Fuli Expressway project. Selected construction contractors for the Danjin project will also join as social capital contributors with RMB458.69 million, lifting Danjin’s registered capital by RMB586.86 million to RMB5.83 billion and slightly diluting Jiangsu Expressway’s stake but strengthening the project’s funding base.

The board confirmed that the capital increase, following a larger RMB2.64 billion pro rata injection earlier in 2025, is neither a related party transaction nor a material asset restructuring under Shanghai and Hong Kong listing rules. The move underscores Jiangsu Expressway’s continued build-out of its toll road portfolio and signals steady progress on a key regional corridor, with implications for long-term traffic growth, earnings contribution, and alignment with local transport development plans.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0177) stock is a Hold with a HK$10.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Jiangsu Expressway Co stock, see the HK:0177 Stock Forecast page.

More about Jiangsu Expressway Co

Jiangsu Expressway Company Limited is a PRC-based toll road operator focused on the investment, construction, and operation of expressways and related infrastructure in Jiangsu province. The company generates revenue primarily from toll collections on its expressway network and partners with local government-backed entities to develop regional transportation links.
